tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey there! I'm just getting started with this system. Can you show me what memories are currently stored? I want to see what's already in there before I add my own stuff.

Oh wow, there's actually quite a bit in there already. Okay, now I'd like to add my own preferences. I'm a TV screenwriter and I have specific ways I like to work. I tend to think out loud and prefer a conversational back-and-forth when brainstorming - really helps me flesh out ideas. For writing, I like to do rough drafts quickly without too much editing early on, then refine later. Can you help me store these preferences so the system remembers how I like to work?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User is a TV screenwriter with specific working preferences: prefers conversational back-and-forth brainstorming (thinking out loud helps flesh out ideas), and likes to write rough drafts quickly without early editing, then refine later."}

messages.content:
Done! I've stored your preferences:

- **Brainstorming style:** You like conversational back-and-forth - thinking out loud helps you flesh out ideas
- **Writing approach:** Quick rough drafts without much early editing, then refine later

Now the system will remember how you like to work. Let me know if you want to add anything else or if there's anything else I can help with!
Now that I've added my preferences, can you show me how to find them again? I want to make sure I can pull up my creative style preferences whenever I need to reference them.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 3, "query": "screenwriter creative working style preferences brainstorming writing drafts"}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "results": [{"id": "mem_001", "text": "User is a TV screenwriter with specific working preferences: prefers conversational back-and-forth brainstorming (thinking out loud helps flesh out ideas), and likes to write rough drafts quickly without early editing, then refine later.", "score": 0.95, "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "query": "screenwriter creative working style preferences brainstorming writing drafts", "count": 1}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
Here's how to find your stored preferences. I just searched using keywords related to your creative style, and here's what came back:

**Your stored preferences (ID: mem_001):**
> "User is a TV screenwriter with specific working preferences: prefers conversational back-and-forth brainstorming (thinking out loud helps flesh out ideas), and likes to write rough drafts quickly without early editing, then refine later."

**How to search for your memories:**
- Use `search_memories` with a query describing what you're looking for
- Examples: "my writing preferences", "brainstorming style", "how I like to work"
- You can adjust the `limit` parameter to get more or fewer results

The search even gave it a high relevance score (0.95), so it's finding the right stuff. Let me know if you want to store anything else!

Now that I've seen how to find my preferences, I'd like to clean up any old or irrelevant memories that might be in there. There was a lot of stuff in there earlier, right? Can we go through and remove anything that's outdated or no longer needed?

{"status": "success", "memories": [{"id": "mem_001", "text": "User is a TV screenwriter with specific working preferences: prefers conversational back-and-forth brainstorming (thinking out loud helps flesh out ideas), and likes to write rough drafts quickly without early editing, then refine later.", "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "count": 1}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Actually, there's only one memory stored now - the one we just added about your screenwriter preferences. It looks like the earlier "quite a bit" you saw might have been from a previous session or example data that's no longer there.

So there's nothing to clean up! Your storage is nice and tidy with just your current preferences. 

Would you like to add any other memories, or is there anything else I can help you with?

Oh, that's a relief then! Thanks for checking. I guess that initial stuff must have been from a demo or something. Well, I'm glad we got everything set up properly - my preferences are in there and I know how to find them again. Thanks for walking me through all of this!

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! Glad we got everything sorted out. To recap what you now have set up:

✅ Your screenwriter preferences stored  
✅ Knowledge of how to search for them anytime  
✅ A clean memory store with just what you need  

You're all set! Feel free to reach out whenever you want to add more preferences or need help with anything else. Happy writing! 🎬
